text.title: Glossary Management as Code Core Rules
text.summary: 8 testable rules with rationale + source for glossary-management-as-code. Includes terminal skip-this-methodology rule.

rule.statement: Glossary MUST live in a single `glossary.yaml` (or split by domain) in the repo root; markdown / wiki copies are derived artefacts only.
rule.rationale: Single-source-of-truth practice — applies to Glossary Management as Code.

rule.statement: Each term MUST carry: canonical_form, definition, synonyms[], deprecated_aliases[], owner, last_reviewed, related[].
rule.rationale: Glossary management research — applies to Glossary Management as Code.

rule.statement: Every glossary change MUST go through a PR; the PR description MUST cite the trigger (new feature, audit finding, term collision).
rule.rationale: Change-control discipline — applies to Glossary Management as Code.

rule.statement: Each term MUST have a single named owner — never 'the team'; un-owned terms auto-flag for cleanup quarterly.
rule.rationale: Single-owner accountability — applies to Glossary Management as Code.

rule.statement: Each term carries a last_reviewed ISO date; terms not reviewed in 6 months are auto-flagged stale and routed to the owner.
rule.rationale: Operational hygiene — applies to Glossary Management as Code.

rule.statement: Removed terms are moved to deprecated_aliases of their replacement; outright deletion is forbidden so old artefacts remain interpretable.
rule.rationale: Schema-evolution research — applies to Glossary Management as Code.

rule.statement: If term A names B as synonym, B's record MUST name A as synonym; asymmetric synonym links are auto-flagged.
rule.rationale: Knowledge-graph hygiene — applies to Glossary Management as Code.

rule.statement: If the project has no recurring vocabulary drift or no PR culture, return `skipped`.
rule.rationale: Methodology applicability — applies to Glossary Management as Code.
